title: Manually arrange list of options in "multi-select question"
excerpt: I’m making a volunteer availability questionnaire and I’d really like to have the options listed as Sunday through Saturday, rather than alphanumerically as seen in the image here.

posts.author: eperkinscrcc (E. Perkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday forms - alpha list2850×1752 162 KB

I’m making a volunteer availability questionnaire and I’d really like to have the options listed as Sunday through Saturday, rather than alphanumerically as seen in the image here.

posts.author: samspacher (Shannon)
posts.cookedHtml: Came here to submit a feature request for this exact issue. Sometimes it’s helpful to have them alphabetized, but it doesn’t always make sense - like with days of the week or listing time slots in chronological order. Please, please, please make this functionality available Monday!!

posts.author: christinakelley (Christina Kelley)
posts.cookedHtml: Was there ever a response from Monday on this? Because this would be really helpful to be able to arrange items manually.

posts.author: jessica1234 (Jessica)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m working on a form now and I really was hoping there was an option to turn off the alphabetic order of these options.

For example, I’ve added a “none of the above” option, but it does not look right because this phrase starts with the letter “N” and due to the auto-ordering of the options, it will not be the last option.

posts.author: MikeB_Axanexa (Mike Buckiewicz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All!

I have used numbers before to keep the order I want in front of the selections. Not ideal but then you can dictate the order with numerical value. Like seen here:

image1230×1062 25 KB

Hope this helps!

Mike B
Automation Architect
